name: Kirsten
subject: broken drug laws
body:
We have to legalize marijuana if we want meaningful criminal justice reform.
| | |
---
Peter,

**We have to legalize marijuana if we want meaningful criminal justice reform.** Every day we fail to take action is another day that communities of color are oppressed by a system that's built on their mass incarceration.
It's shameful that my son would likely be treated very differently from one of his black or Latino peers if he were caught with marijuana. Our country's broken, discriminatory marijuana policies continue to devastate lives-robbing people of job opportunities, of time with their families and of their futures. We can't stay silent while this injustice stares us in the face. **We have to act now to end it.**
We'll do that by passing the Marijuana Justice Act. But pushing this reform forward is going to take all of us speaking out to demand change.**So if you're with us, if you believe we all need to step up in this fight for justice and equality, I'm counting on you to raise your voice right now:**
| | **Should we legalize marijuana?**
---
| | | [YES](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://act.kirstengillibrand.com/kg2020-legalize-1qs?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk3034-su-gfp-nd")
---
| [NO](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://act.kirstengillibrand.com/kg2020-legalize-1qs?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk3034-su-gfp-nd")
---
Thank you for doing your part to help end this systemic discrimination,

Kirsten

body:
Will you sign your name and publicly endorse me to be New Mexico's next Democratic Senator before midnight? It would mean so much to me. >>

| | I wanted to ask you personally: [_**Will you sign your name and publicly endorse me to be New Mexico 's next Democratic Senator before midnight? It would mean so much to me. >> **_](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b58a/18e32a7f/5d50baac/7b8223e1/2105886546/VEsE/ "https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/endorse-benray?source=20190420_em_pet_b1_lujan_all") I'm so proud of the fights we won together last year. I led the charge to take back the House from Republicans' dangerous grasp -- and Speaker Nancy Pelosi and I have spent every second in our new Majority fighting on behalf of the people who put us there. But when I look at the critical progress Mitch McConnell and his Republican Senate are blocking us from making -- I feel compelled to step up. **That 's why I'm running for Senate -- and why the endorsement of real, hardworking families like yours means so much to me.** [_**Over the next few weeks, I 'll publicly release a list of Americans who have lent their endorsement to my Senate campaign. I would love for you to be one of them. >>**_](https://secure.benrlujan.com/page/m/40f6b58a/18e32a7f/5d50baac/7b8223e6/2105886546/VEsF/ "http://secure.benrlujan.com/page/s/endorse-benray?source=20190420_em_pet_b1_lujan_all") Thank you so much for your support. -Ben Ray Lujan

name: Andrew Yang
subject: The Power of Our Ideas
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Andrew Yang
body:
| | Friend --
[](http://my.yang2020.com/?e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=042019_newsletter&n=1) Hello and thank you for your support! It means everything to us. Our latest rally was Thursday night in Atlanta—more than 1,000 amazing people came out. We made many new friends; the [Humanity First Tour](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=c5dAo5qulKAncvNYjq2uGcnHAN92nM1CHInwgA-PIi7HdTRyEez5JIs0AF2zI3zA&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=042019_newsletter&n=2 "Schedule of Rallies on the Humanity First Tour") is a blast and getting bigger and better as we grow. The rally was [covered in the Atlanta Journal Constitution](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=HXZyQpkNurssStlgPluB9JEBTmMUz8s0e0HPP2M9LLlgbsn79LBm8wi1ug5xZZUzIEAXlIR0JnLnoV3ur4AqKkgJvRn5OPyHM8Nkz40fyuh6rMwQKnjhWVSZgB58h-dR-nCLwCeQMMU2Q2siZyuHZof-YJen0iBDsVXXZ_wN0q_8T9KcxRJiULUA0REf2iee&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=042019_newsletter&n=3 "Democrat Yang brings presidential
campaign to Atlanta") the next day. | |
---|---
One benefit of the Tour is all of the press we have been getting at each stop. A couple people have run analyses of the number of press mentions and articles about different presidential candidates. Perhaps not surprisingly, I am in last place.
[](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=qXw0juI4WClSIm49HXDXjLDv-1tQWAGB_BLsbULDMx3G8hqFyl85kFod6EfnS4BnMLk_2QxPmIgRqbQ0LbQOwFAdppiDSNT0EzKLn3kpfOrRwk7M_AlSLZEcHmItsQhWluHqnYPDfnWy1omoWzaZAmIKPqUZx-NcgQRGpw_4xIyAzIuyTFfrabiJXrSZF2-yspbjGEREhtSSPg-RBp5B8KjxyhXcgKJtEgyqOrsBfcE&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=042019_newsletter&n=4 "Which
Democratic Candidate Gets the Most News Coverage?")
What you might not realize is this is a GREAT sign for the campaign.
[We are polling at 3% nationally](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=GYpNoCzTmABToRy0Kj0kLgFdloRUFx_pW-yoTFVRs0gjOdvhrM_fx1RtH-dLcMkrIaVIizyegWej3ckkTPgeFXPh4xYKndi2SjSdfr7XKdCV3NtdD_DYvsYk0H0dXDqHGSDMhaocwdo43skc9trSnmGa5b3YZslRXALewD7w62g&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=042019_newsletter&n=5 "Emerson College Polling in
Democratic Primary Field") despite most people never having heard of me. If we are drawing that support despite low name recognition and little press, that means that as more people find out about us we will do better and better. Indeed, one person ran an analysis on the polling-to-article ratio, and I am well ahead of the field.
This speaks to the power of our campaign. Many of you have been drawn to this campaign by [our specific policies and ideas](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=c5dAo5qulKAncvNYjq2uGaNvpZQglSZsA7euzomQUw1ojb7e3Laf5sGfL6W7fKdf&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=042019_newsletter&n=6 "Andrew Yang's policies page"). We pride ourselves on having a clear vision that we have laid out for the world to see. Of course there is [the Freedom Dividend](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=c5dAo5qulKAncvNYjq2uGaNvpZQglSZsA7euzomQUw2Xph5A9jUY2Oea3WOThNgpstNWeS2t9gcVOX9KZjbOjw&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=042019_newsletter&n=7 "Police page for the Freedom Dividend") and [Human-Centered Capitalism](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=c5dAo5qulKAncvNYjq2uGaNvpZQglSZsA7euzomQUw3ShKZnDwR3U_vZuV9xUlvs9G-tpFTbRKg438FZ527Uqw&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=042019_newsletter&n=8 "Policy page for Human-Centered
Capitalism"), but there are also [banning robocalls](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=c5dAo5qulKAncvNYjq2uGaNvpZQglSZsA7euzomQUw2zbLmLXr0qxRR_fD2_Lu4W_Efrug6kZoVdt_6A9VSzgA&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=042019_newsletter&n=9 "Policy page for banning
robo-calls"), [a psychologist in the White House](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=c5dAo5qulKAncvNYjq2uGaNvpZQglSZsA7euzomQUw0Gr7ZFn7IWSChEqVYSiK6i6QyJUr092NOWnWewF6JgDA&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=042019_newsletter&n=10 "Policy page for a White House psychologist"), [paying NCAA athletes](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=c5dAo5qulKAncvNYjq2uGaNvpZQglSZsA7euzomQUw22aTTyUxObaxFjYtmrxAO2igne2vPO98xw61xTudUcQg&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=042019_newsletter&n=11 "Policy page for paying

name: James Mackler
subject: Why I support medical marijuana
disclaimer_text: Paid for by James Mackler for U
body:
Hundreds of thousands of Americans struggle with PTSD, addiction, and chronic pain. For some, current forms of treatment are ineffective and others carry a laundry list of dangerous and life-threateni
|
---
[ ](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa283/18d6a294/4ace0698/26d6a4d6/3967964333/VEsH/ "https://www.jamesmackler.com/")

| | Hundreds of thousands of Americans struggle with PTSD, addiction, and chronic pain. For some, current forms of treatment are ineffective and others carry a laundry list of dangerous and life-threatening side effects. **Some of these individuals are veterans who are struggling to manage injuries related to their service,** whether physical, emotional, or psychological. Which is part of the reason why the vast majority of veterans and their families are in favor of increasing research into and legalizing medical marijuana. When I'm asked about the legalization of medical marijuana I think about this statement from former Marine and two-time Purple Heart recipient Joshua James Frey: **_" Without medical marijuana I would be dead."_** For many like Joshua, it helps them manage the symptoms associated with PTSD and chronic pain injuries. If a doctor believes that their patient can benefit from medical marijuana, then it isn't any politician's place to stop them. [**_Join me and call for the legalization of medical marijuana >>_**](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa283/18d6a294/4ace0698/26d6a4d7/3967964333/VEsE/ "https://secure.jamesmackler.com/e190420") Medical marijuana has also demonstrated promise in regards to managing symptoms resulting from epilepsy and chemotherapy. If legalizing medical marijuana can help manage a range of conditions that millions of Americans have -- then there isn't a question about it, we need to legalize medical marijuana. [**_Sign your name if you are in support of legalizing medical marijuana >>_**](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa283/18d6a294/4ace0698/26d6a4d7/3967964333/VEsF/ "https://secure.jamesmackler.com/e190420") | | | [Add Your Name](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa283/18d6a294/4ace0698/26d6a4d7/3967964333/VEsC/ "https://secure.jamesmackler.com/e190420")
---
Thanks,
James

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Resolute Reads
body:
"President Trump has every right to feel liberated. What the [ report shows is that he endured a special-counsel probe..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=77c6d7cacf&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **_REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS_**
---
| | | [Mueller’s Report Speaks Volumes](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=58cd1f0ae2&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Wall Street Journal_
“President Trump has every right to feel liberated. What the [Mueller] report shows is that he endured a special-counsel probe that was relentlessly, at times farcically, obsessed with taking him out,” Kimberly Strassel writes. “What stands out is just how diligently and creatively the special counsel’s legal minds worked to implicate someone in Trump World on something Russia- or obstruction-of-justice-related. And how—even with all its overweening power and aggressive tactics—it still struck out.”

**🎬****Watch:** [NO COLLUSION – NO OBSTRUCTION!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a1784198ab&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [Mainstream Media Doubles Down on Trump Conspiracies](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fb701c2bfc&e=3ee4985eea)
-_New York Post_
“Mainstream media coverage of the Russia-collusion story has been dead wrong for two years, yet the effort to smear President Trump continues — even after Thursday’s release of the Mueller report,” David Harsanyi writes. “In a responsible media environment, journalists who had been misled by their partisan sources for years would be clamoring to find out how the entire country could be plunged into a 22-month-long destabilizing investigation that amounted to nothing more than a now-debunked conspiracy theory.”

**🎬 Attorney General Barr:[No collusion. That’s the bottom line.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3c95efdaca&e=3ee4985eea)**
---
|
---
| [The Mueller Report Just Left Egg on Democrats’ Faces](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ebde15bdef&e=3ee4985eea)
-_New York Post_
“Trump’s election is now the most verifiably legitimate election in history. No other campaign has endured the kind of scrutiny that the Trump team has withstood. For nearly two years, 19 lawyers and 40 investigators questioned some 500 witnesses worldwide. They issued 500 search warrants and 2,800 subpoenas,” Abe Greenwald writes. “There was no underlying crime.”
---
|
---
| [Mueller Probe Was a Waste of Time and Money – No Collusion, No Obstruction](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b1c22d194d&e=3ee4985eea)
-Fox News
“As partisan Democrats and the liberal media hyperventilate over Attorney General William Barr’s release of Special Counsel Robert Mueller’s report Thursday, they’re willfully ignoring the fact that they already know what the report concludes: Two years and $35 million was spent investigating a lie,” David Bossie writes.
---
|
---
| [Vice President Mike Pence: Congress Must Act to End the Crisis on Our Border](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e87a7936ca&e=3ee4985eea)
-Fox News
“This week, Mayor Douglas Nicholls of Yuma, Arizona, declared a state of emergency as thousands of illegal immigrants poured into the city and pushed it to its breaking point. As the mayor warned, the sudden influx of people is ‘above our capacity as a community to sustain,’” Vice President Mike Pence writes. “It’s time for Congress to do its job and close the loopholes in our immigration laws that are driving the humanitarian and security crisis on our southern border.”

**UPDATE:** [New Mexico county asks governor to send in the National Guard ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7c391f58cf&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [The Tax Cut that Keeps on Giving](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=cac0161489&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Washington Times_
“Another Tax Day is behind us, but negative media coverage of President Trump’s signature tax-cut legislation continues,” Heritage Foundation founder Ed Feulner writes. “Many reporters and pundits didn’t even wait until the tax cut was signed into law in December 2017 to begin mischaracterizing it . . . Never mind that the real-world experience of most Americans in the wake of these articles contradicted these gloom-and-doom predictions.”
---
|
---
| [Democrats, As Usual, Were Wrong on Trump’s Tax Cuts](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3651b40208&e=3ee4985eea)
-Fox News
In honor of Tax Day this past week, “let’s look back at the predictions that were made when President Donald Trump signed the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act around Christmas 2017,” Rep. Kevin Brady (R-TX) writes. Now-Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-CA) infamously predicted that America should brace for “Armageddon.” Liberal economist-turned-columnist Paul Krugman guaranteed a “global recession, with no end in sight.”
---
|
---
| [Strong Jobs Market Spurs Bigger Salary Offers](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ff81405464&e=3ee4985eea)
-_MarketWatch_
“Americans still think plenty of jobs are available and companies are offering better pay as they compete for a shrinking pool of available labor, new study by the New York Federal Reserve shows,” Jeffry Bartash reports. “The Fed study is the latest proof the jobs market continues to sizzle. The rate of layoffs each week — known as jobless claims — fell below 200,000 earlier this month for the first time since 1969.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d8861aa562&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0544ae3911&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=b0748f08e7)
---

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: I wouldn't ask if it weren't powerfully important: Will you make your first donation to our campaign?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
Without the backing of wealthy donors, I truly need your help to make sure the ideas we care about continue to be part of the national conversation. | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852fc37/45c30650/b7270bbe/66970f8f/3747788301/VEsH/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/")
---
There's only one way we're raising money for this campaign.

I'm not holding any fundraisers where you can only get in by writing a big check -- because a person's wealth shouldn't determine the amount of time they can spend with me -- or any presidential candidate.

I'm not counting on a billionaire to fund a Super PAC for me -- because the Democratic primary can't just be one more plaything for the rich and powerful.

I'm not taking a dime from federally registered lobbyists, corporate PACs, or PACs of any kind -- because our campaign is about people, not powerful special interests.

**Instead, we're building a true grassroots campaign with grassroots donors -- because that's the only way we'll create a movement strong enough to make big, structural change in our economy, our government, and our democracy.**

**[I wouldn't ask if it weren't powerfully important: Will you make your first donation to our campaign? $3 or whatever you can chip in would make a huge difference. I can't do this without you.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852fc37/45c30650/b7270bbe/66970f8e/3747788301/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LWFwcjE5ZnIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IjIwMTkwNDIwZW0xLWZ1bGwifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ew-apr19fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=20190420em1-full")**

On a typical presidential campaign, my team would be flying me from big city to big city to scoop up as many big fat checks as possible.

Wealthy donors might come see me at fancy receptions in hedge fund offices. They'd sip champagne, nibble on shrimp cocktail, and try to convince me that the tax loophole that just so happens to pad their pockets is also good for the whole country.

But I'm not running to be a president who only pays attention to the wealthy and well-connected.

I'm running to be everybody's president. My time is not for sale, and every supporter has an equal place in our movement.

Instead of getting cozy with the rich and powerful, I'm in New Hampshire this weekend for meet-and-greets and organizing events. Anyone's welcome to come -- no big check required.

If you ask me, that's what a campaign for president should be all about. Talking with everyday Americans, in their hometowns, about real solutions to real problems. Getting organized at the grassroots level to make change happen. Having conversations with school-teachers, bus operators, McDonald's workers (hello, Logan in Idaho!), and anyone who gives a little at a time.

But this type of campaign is only possible if everyone who is able pitches in what they can.

I'm running to be the best president that money can't buy, but I can't do it alone. This is how we'll stop Washington from just answering to the beck and call of billionaires and giant corporations -- one grassroots donation at a time.

**Without the backing of wealthy donors, I truly need your help to make sure the ideas we care about continue to be part of the national conversation.**

**[Please make your first contribution to own a piece of this campaign. Let's build something incredible together.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852fc37/45c30650/b7270bbe/66970f8e/3747788301/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LWFwcjE5ZnIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IjIwMTkwNDIwZW0xLWZ1bGwifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ew-apr19fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=20190420em1-full")**

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth

-> Make Election Day a national holiday
-> Dismantle unfair gerrymandering laws
-> Establish universal registration_ Booker says he wants to ensure that groups hurt by voter suppression, particularly people of color, are able to make their voices heard. Here at the Voter Protection Project, we love to hear any and all new ideas that will expand the right to vote across the nation. **[But we want to know what you think: take our one-click poll to let us know if you support Booker's new proposal >>>](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d077c/72412298/446359aa/610144d7/294046761/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG1WdFlXbHNJSDE5IjoiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iLCJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG5wcGNDQjlmUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/booker?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=1+-+But+we+want+to+know+what+you+think+take+&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_190420_Booker_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_190420_Booker_U1_X1")** | | | [TAKE THE POLL >> ](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d077c/72412298/446359aa/610144d6/294046761/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG1WdFlXbHNJSDE5IjoiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iLCJlM3NnY21WamFYQnBaVzUwTG5wcGNDQjlmUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/booker?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=2+-+TAKE+THE+POLL+gtgt+&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_190420_Booker_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_190420_Booker_U1_X1")

name: John Hickenlooper
subject: 20 years
body:
A lot can change in 20 years. I wish it would. | | | [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771683/691c9bd/6cbc666c/11a4a82f/3483218299/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-em-apr?refcode=em_template")
---
| Friend, Today marks 20 years since the mass shooting at Columbine High School in Littleton, Colorado, on the south side of metro Denver. A lot can change in 20 years. It _should_ change. We _shouldn 't_ know the words Sandy Hook, Tree of Life, Mother Emanuel, Pulse, or Parkland--not for the reasons we do. Yet there they are, part of our shared vocabulary of frustration and grief.

Just this week, Denver-area schools were shut down because of threats of gun violence. Half a million kids--including students at Columbine High--were kept home on Wednesday, traumatized again by an all too familiar threat.

_**Enough.**_

Here in Colorado, we stood up to the NRA. After the mass shooting in Aurora, we responded by coming together to ban high capacity magazines, make investments in mental health, and enact universal background checks. These are all critical first steps that have helped save lives, but they are not sufficient.

**In our state and across our country, we can and must do more.** We can raise the required age for gun purchases; we can pass red flag laws. That spirit of action that moved us in Colorado needs to become the norm in Washington.

We can't look back again, another 20 years from now, having allowed countless more names--of schools, places of worship, or public spaces --to become part of our lexicon of grief.

Today, the day we remember the tragedy at Columbine High School, I invite you to join me in renewing our national commitment to eradicate this epidemic--not just through the repetition of "thoughts and prayers," but through listening, learning, and working toward real legislation that makes a _permanent_ difference.

Thank you,

John

---

name: Andrew Romanoff
subject: Columbine
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROMANOFF FOR COLORADO
body:
But for those who survived that tragedy, whose loved ones were slain, whose lives were shattered, those memories are ever-present. | [ ](https://secure.andrewromanoff.com/page/m/7a984a90/33d1dea4/3b8ed952/3a3a76e0/3960172876/VEsH/ "http://www.andrewromanoff.com")
---
| | | Today brings back memories of Columbine. What was then the deadliest school shooting in American history took place on April 20, 1999. But for those who survived that tragedy, whose loved ones were slain, whose lives were shattered, those memories are ever-present. We should do everything we can to ease their pain and to spare other families the anguish they suffer still. That includes taking action to reduce the risk of gun violence. Tom Mauser has led that fight, in Colorado and across the country, for the last 20 years. Tom lost his son Daniel at Columbine; he's worn Daniel's shoes ever since. I first met Tom when we worked together to close the gun-show loophole--a measure the voters approved in 2000 after the state legislature refused. I remain awed by his courage. A year after his son's death, Tom decided to listen to a 911 tape of the shooting. "I felt compelled to listen," he said later, "so that I could hear the sounds Daniel heard in his last moments of life." The victims' screams are punctuated by gunfire, and by their killers' cries of delight. It was, Tom said, "one of the most disturbing experiences of my lifetime." At 11:34 a.m., a shot rang out; the bullet pierced Daniel's hand. The second shot came two seconds later, likely killing him instantly. "Could there possibly be a more horrific, solitary sound that a parent might hear?" Tom asked. Perhaps only the sound of our silence. Andrew Romanoff
